<<<KOT:uremic>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"uremic is a medical-technical adjective denoting a pathophysiological state (excess urea and metabolic waste products in blood due to kidney dysfunction). Its identity conditions are grounded in biochemistry, organ physiology, and clinical laboratory measurement — domains not expressible in the prime lexicon or via the available catalog concepts. It requires technical medical knowledge and belongs to the medical/scientific sector, not the everyday-sense tier."}
<<<END:uremic>>>>

<<<KOT:wagnerian>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"wagnerian is a proper-adjective derived from Wilhelm Richard Wagner, a historical person and composer. It denotes the aesthetic, compositional, and philosophical style associated with Wagner's operas and music dramas—concepts (leitmotif, Gesamtkunstwerk, continuous sung narrative, romantic opera revolution) that belong to music theory, art history, and culture-specific aesthetics. These require domain knowledge far beyond the prime lexicon and cannot be grounded in everyday perceptual or bodily terms."}
<<<END:wagnerian>>>>
